evals.prompt: I want to add MatrixScan Batch to my Android app. Here's my empty Activity: EmptyActivity.kt. I need to scan EAN-13 and QR barcodes. Please show me the complete updated Activity code in your response.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads integration.md, fetches the latest SDK version, returns complete BarcodeBatch integration code inline (DataCaptureContext, Camera, BarcodeBatch.forDataCaptureContext, DataCaptureView, BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay, BarcodeBatchListener with onSessionUpdated, lifecycle), and shows the setup checklist.

assertions.text: Setup checklist is shown and mentions adding com.scandit.datacapture:barcode and com.scandit.datacapture:core Gradle dependencies
Setup checklist mentions adding CAMERA permission to AndroidManifest.xml
Setup checklist mentions requesting CAMERA permission at runtime
A license key placeholder string is present
A concrete version number is used for the dependencies (not a [version] placeholder)
DataCaptureContext.forLicenseKey( is used
BarcodeBatchSettings is constructed and configured
Symbology.EAN13_UPCA is enabled
Symbology.QR is enabled
Camera.getDefaultCamera(BarcodeBatch.createRecommendedCameraSettings()) is used
dataCaptureContext.setFrameSource(camera) is called
BarcodeBatch.forDataCaptureContext( is used (not a direct BarcodeBatch constructor)
barcodeBatch.addListener( is called
DataCaptureView.newInstance( is used and set as the content view
BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay.newInstance( is called
BarcodeBatchListener is implemented with onSessionUpdated
onSessionUpdated signature has parameters (mode: BarcodeBatch, session: BarcodeBatchSession, data: FrameData)
onSessionUpdated dispatches UI work via runOnUiThread
onResume turns the camera on (switchToDesiredState(FrameSourceState.ON))
onPause turns the camera off (switchToDesiredState(FrameSourceState.OFF))
barcodeBatch.removeListener( is called in onDestroy
dataCaptureContext.removeCurrentMode() is called in onDestroy

evals.prompt: I want to highlight EAN-13 barcodes in green and CODE128 barcodes in blue in my MatrixScan Batch Activity. Here is my Activity: IntegratedActivity.kt
evals.expected_output: The skill implements BarcodeBatchBasicOverlayListener, overrides brushForTrackedBarcode to return colored brushes per symbology, and assigns the listener to the overlay.

assertions.text: BarcodeBatchBasicOverlayListener is implemented
brushForTrackedBarcode is overridden
EAN13_UPCA returns a green Brush
CODE128 returns a blue Brush
overlay.listener = this (or equivalent) is assigned
Existing integration code (BarcodeBatch, DataCaptureView, BarcodeBatchListener, camera lifecycle) is preserved

evals.prompt: In my MatrixScan Batch Activity I want to log all currently tracked barcodes — their tracking ID and data — on every frame. Here is my Activity: IntegratedActivity.kt
evals.expected_output: The skill updates onSessionUpdated to iterate over session.trackedBarcodes, log each TrackedBarcode's identifier and barcode.data.

assertions.text: session.trackedBarcodes is accessed
Each TrackedBarcode's identifier is used
Each TrackedBarcode's barcode.data is used
Data is copied out of the session before runOnUiThread (session.trackedBarcodes is not accessed inside the UI lambda)
Existing integration code (BarcodeBatch factory, camera setup, overlay, lifecycle) is preserved

evals.prompt: In my MatrixScan Batch Activity I'd rather show a dot over each tracked barcode instead of the default rectangular frame. Here is my Activity: IntegratedActivity.kt
evals.expected_output: The skill switches the basic overlay to the DOT style by using the three-argument BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay.newInstance overload with BarcodeBatchBasicOverlayStyle.DOT, and preserves the rest of the integration.

assertions.text: BarcodeBatchBasicOverlayStyle.DOT is passed to BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay.newInstance(
The three-argument BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay.newInstance(barcodeBatch, dataCaptureView, style) overload is used
BarcodeBatchBasicOverlayStyle is imported from com.scandit.datacapture.barcode.batch.ui.overlay
The overlay style is not changed by assigning overlay.style (style is read-only and set at creation) (semantic)
Existing integration code (BarcodeBatch factory, camera setup, BarcodeBatchListener, lifecycle) is preserved

evals.prompt: When the user taps a highlighted barcode in my MatrixScan Batch Activity, I want to show a Toast with that barcode's data. Here is my Activity: IntegratedActivity.kt
evals.expected_output: The skill implements BarcodeBatchBasicOverlayListener, overrides onTrackedBarcodeTapped to read trackedBarcode.barcode.data and show a Toast, and assigns the listener to the overlay.

assertions.text: BarcodeBatchBasicOverlayListener is implemented
onTrackedBarcodeTapped is overridden
onTrackedBarcodeTapped signature has parameters (overlay: BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay, trackedBarcode: TrackedBarcode)
trackedBarcode.barcode.data is read inside onTrackedBarcodeTapped
The iOS/JS callback name didTapTrackedBarcode is NOT used (semantic)
overlay.listener = this (or equivalent) is assigned
Existing integration code (BarcodeBatch factory, camera setup, BarcodeBatchListener, lifecycle) is preserved

evals.prompt: I want to overlay a custom Android label (a TextView showing the barcode's data) on top of each tracked barcode in my MatrixScan Batch Activity. Here is my Activity: IntegratedActivity.kt
evals.expected_output: The skill adds a BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay via newInstance, implements BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlayListener.viewForTrackedBarcode to return an Android View (TextView) per barcode, and assigns the listener. It notes the MatrixScan AR add-on requirement.

assertions.text: BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay.newInstance(barcodeBatch, dataCaptureView) is used
BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlayListener is implemented
viewForTrackedBarcode is overridden and returns an android.view.View (e.g. a TextView)
viewForTrackedBarcode signature has parameters (overlay: BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay, trackedBarcode: TrackedBarcode)
The returned View uses trackedBarcode.barcode.data as its text
advancedOverlay.listener = this (or equivalent) is assigned
The MatrixScan AR add-on requirement is mentioned
Existing integration code (BarcodeBatch factory, camera setup, BarcodeBatchListener, lifecycle) is preserved

evals.prompt: I already show a custom view via BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay in my MatrixScan Batch Activity, but it sits on top of the barcode. I want it anchored to the top-center of the barcode and offset upward so it floats just above the code. Here is my Activity: IntegratedActivity.kt
evals.expected_output: The skill overrides anchorForTrackedBarcode to return Anchor.TOP_CENTER and offsetForTrackedBarcode to return a PointWithUnit built from FloatWithUnit values in MeasureUnit.FRACTION (e.g. y = -1f) so the view floats above the barcode.

assertions.text: anchorForTrackedBarcode is overridden and returns Anchor.TOP_CENTER
offsetForTrackedBarcode is overridden and returns a PointWithUnit
The PointWithUnit is constructed from FloatWithUnit values (not DoubleWithUnit, which is the Flutter name) (semantic)
MeasureUnit.FRACTION is used for the offset unit
Anchor is imported from com.scandit.datacapture.core.common.geometry
offsetForTrackedBarcode signature has parameters (overlay: BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay, trackedBarcode: TrackedBarcode, view: View)
Existing advanced overlay and integration code is preserved

evals.prompt: In my MatrixScan Batch Activity I want to remove a barcode from my on-screen list as soon as it leaves the camera view. Here is my Activity: IntegratedActivity.kt
evals.expected_output: The skill reads session.removedTrackedBarcodes in onSessionUpdated, copies the list of tracking identifiers out of the session, and removes matching entries from the app's own state keyed by tracking ID. It explains removedTrackedBarcodes contains only Int identifiers.

assertions.text: session.removedTrackedBarcodes is accessed in onSessionUpdated
removedTrackedBarcodes is treated as a list of Int tracking identifiers (not TrackedBarcode objects) (semantic)
The removed tracking IDs are used to remove entries from the app's own collection
The removed IDs are copied out of the session before runOnUiThread (session is not accessed inside the UI lambda)
Existing integration code (BarcodeBatch factory, camera setup, overlay, lifecycle) is preserved

evals.prompt: I want my MatrixScan Batch Activity to beep and vibrate each time a new barcode starts being tracked. Here is my Activity: IntegratedActivity.kt
evals.expected_output: The skill explains BarcodeBatch has no automatic feedback and emits it manually: creates a single reusable Feedback (Feedback.defaultFeedback()) and calls emit() in onSessionUpdated when session.addedTrackedBarcodes is non-empty.

assertions.text: The answer explains BarcodeBatch does not emit feedback automatically and it must be emitted manually (semantic)
Feedback.defaultFeedback() is used to create the Feedback instance
Feedback is imported from com.scandit.datacapture.core.feedback
feedback.emit() is called inside onSessionUpdated
emit() is gated on session.addedTrackedBarcodes being non-empty (so it fires only on newly tracked barcodes)
A single Feedback instance is created once and reused (not constructed every frame) (semantic)
Existing integration code (BarcodeBatch factory, camera setup, overlay, lifecycle) is preserved
